Legal

Cookie Policy

This policy explains the limited browser storage and third-party resources used by the SteadyDesk IT website, including what your cookie-banner choice currently does.

Last updated: July 21, 2026No analytics or advertising trackers currently active

On this page

Scope Technology we use Your choices Third-party resources Browser controls Contact us
Current setup

The Site does not currently use analytics, advertising, or behavior-tracking cookies. It stores only your cookie-banner choice in your browser's local storage. Choosing "Accept all" records that preference but does not activate optional trackers because none are currently installed.

1. Scope of this policy

This Cookie Policy applies to steadydeskit.com, operated by SteadyDesk IT Services LLC ("SteadyDesk IT," "we," "us," or "our"). It should be read with our Privacy Policy, which explains how we handle information submitted through the Site.

2. Cookies and similar technologies

A cookie is a small text file a website or service provider can save through your browser. Similar technologies include local storage, pixels, software-development kits, and device identifiers. They can support essential functions, remember choices, measure use, or deliver advertising.

Local storage is browser-based storage associated with a website. Unlike a traditional cookie, its contents are not automatically included in every request to the web server. The Site uses local storage only for the privacy preference described below.

3. Technology currently used

Name or providerTypePurposeDuration
steadydesk_cookie_preferenceEssential local storageRemembers whether you selected "Accept all" or "Essential only," together with the date and time the choice was saved.Until you change the choice or clear this Site's browser data.
Hosting and security infrastructureEssential request processingDelivers pages, routes form submissions, maintains availability, and helps detect errors or abuse. Providers may process ordinary request information and may use strictly necessary identifiers depending on the hosting configuration.Varies by provider and security need.
Lucide icon resourceThird-party content requestLoads the icon library used in the Site interface. The content-delivery provider receives normal network request information needed to return that file.Subject to the provider's delivery and logging practices.

The Site does not currently set up analytics measurement, advertising pixels, cross-site tracking, or personalization profiles. We will update this policy and, where required, request an appropriate choice before adding non-essential technologies.

4. How the banner choices work

  • Essential only: saves that choice so the banner stays closed. The Site continues to use only functionality needed to deliver pages, remember the choice, protect the Site, and receive a form submission.
  • Accept all: saves your broader preference. Because no optional analytics or advertising tools are currently installed, this choice does not enable additional tracking at this time.
  • Cookie settings: the footer link reopens the banner so you can replace your saved choice at any time.

Changing a preference affects future Site behavior. It does not erase server records already created for legitimate security, troubleshooting, or legal purposes.

5. Third-party resources and links

Some Site resources may be delivered through third-party infrastructure, and the Site may link to external services. Those providers can receive technical request information such as an IP address, browser details, requested file, and request time. Their processing is governed by their own policies. Merely following an external link may also allow that destination to set its own cookies, which SteadyDesk IT does not control.

6. Browser and device controls

You can use the Site's "Cookie settings" link to replace your saved choice. You can also inspect, block, or delete cookies and local storage through your browser's privacy or site-data settings. The exact steps differ by browser and device. Clearing Site data removes the stored preference, so the banner will appear again on a later visit.

Blocking all storage may prevent the Site from remembering that you dismissed the banner. Browser privacy signals such as Global Privacy Control or Do Not Track do not change additional advertising behavior here because the Site does not currently sell personal information or use targeted-advertising trackers.

7. Changes to this policy

We may update this Cookie Policy if the Site, hosting arrangement, service providers, or legal requirements change. The "Last updated" date identifies the current version. If we introduce technology that requires consent, we will update the banner or provide another appropriate notice before activating it where required.

8. Contact us

Questions about browser storage or this policy may be sent to SteadyDesk IT Services LLC at support@steadydeskit.com or 201 W 5th Street, Suite 1100, Austin, TX 78701.

Reliable day-to-day IT help, safer systems, and clear guidance for small teams without an internal system administrator.

Remote support for small businesses across the United States.
